QF.id: IV Clearing Settlement and Risk Management_13

question.text: A clearing member's collateral utilization reaches 92%. What action does the exchange take?

answer.text: All unexecuted orders of the member are cancelled
The member receives a warning but can continue trading
Only new buy orders are restricted
The member's account is suspended for 24 hours

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** When collateral utilization reaches 90%, all unexecuted orders of the clearing member are cancelled by the exchange. At 92%, which exceeds the 90% threshold, this action is triggered.
**The Trap:** A warning system exists at lower thresholds, not at 90%+. Selective order cancellation is not the protocol - ALL unexecuted orders are cancelled. Suspension is a separate, more severe action.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The 90% collateral utilization trigger is consistent across derivative segments regulated by SEBI, including equity and currency derivatives.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Members should monitor collateral utilization in real-time and top up margins well before the 90% threshold to avoid disruptive order cancellations.
